name: Registration Entry Tampering
description: Create, modify, and delete SPIRE registration entries to manipulate workload identity assignment
real_world_relevance: Compromise of SPIRE Server admin access leading to arbitrary workload impersonation

attack_flow.description: Attacker with SPIRE Server access manipulates registration entries to impersonate workloads
diagram: 1. Attacker gains access to SPIRE Server admin socket
2. Creates rogue registration entry for target workload identity
3. Fetches SVID using the rogue entry
4. Uses stolen identity to access services as the target workload

actions.description: Create entry impersonating admin-service with root selector

phases.name: Entry Modification
phases.description: Modify existing entries to widen access

actions.description: List all entries to find modification targets

phases.name: Track Covering
phases.description: Delete rogue entries to hide evidence

actions.description: Delete rogue entry after obtaining SVID

indicators_of_compromise.description: Registration tampering followed by SVID acquisition

expected_wazuh_alerts.description: NHI: SPIRE registration entry modified or deleted
NHI: Registration tampering + SVID acquisition chain

immediate: Audit all registration entries for unauthorized changes
Revoke SVIDs issued to rogue entries
long_term: Restrict SPIRE Server admin socket access
Forward SPIRE Server audit logs to SIEM
Use OPA/Rego policies for entry validation
